info.title: CSharpier
info.description: CSharpier is an opinionated code formatter for c#.

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information

options.name: -h
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --no-cache
options.description: Bypass the cache to determine if a file needs to be formatted.

options.name: --no-msbuild-check
options.description: Bypass the check to determine if a csproj files references a different version of CSharpier.MsBuild.

options.name: --include-generated
options.description: Include files generated by the SDK and files that begin with <autogenerated /> comments

options.name: --skip-validation
options.description: Skip validation of formatted files to improve performance.

options.name: --skip-write
options.description: Skip writing changes. Generally used for testing to ensure csharpier doesn't throw any errors or cause syntax tree validation failures.

options.name: --write-stdout
options.description: Write the results of formatting any files to stdout.

options.name: --compilation-errors-as-warnings
options.description: Treat compilation errors from files as warnings instead of errors.

options.name: --config-path
options.description: Path to the CSharpier configuration file

options.name: --ignore-path
options.description: Path to the CSharpier ignore file

options.name: --stdin-path
options.description: Supply a path when piping input to format via stdin

options.name: --log-format
options.description:
Log output format
Console (default) - Formats messages in a human readable way for console interaction.
MsBuild - Formats messages in standard error/warning format for MSBuild.

options.name: --log-level
options.description: Specify the log level - Debug, Information (default), Warning, Error, None

arguments.name: DIRECTORYORFILE
arguments.description: One or more paths to - a directory containing files to format or a specific file to format. May be omitted when piping data via stdin.

commands.name: check
commands.description: Check that files are formatted. Will not write any changes.

options.name: --config-path
options.description: Path to the CSharpier configuration file

options.name: --ignore-path
options.description: Path to the CSharpier ignore file

options.name: --log-format
options.description:
Log output format
Console (default) - Formats messages in a human readable way for console interaction.
MsBuild - Formats messages in standard error/warning format for MSBuild.

options.name: --log-level
options.description: Specify the log level - Debug, Information (default), Warning, Error, None

options.name: --include-generated
options.description: Include files generated by the SDK and files that begin with <autogenerated /> comments

options.name: --no-msbuild-check
options.description: Bypass the check to determine if a csproj files references a different version of CSharpier.MsBuild.

options.name: --compilation-errors-as-warnings
options.description: Treat compilation errors from files as warnings instead of errors.

options.name: --unformatted-as-warnings
options.description: Treat unformatted files as warnings instead of errors.

arguments.name: DIRECTORYORFILE
arguments.description: One or more paths to - a directory containing files to format or a specific file to format. May be omitted when piping data via stdin.

commands.name: pipe-files
commands.description: Keep csharpier running so that multiples files can be piped to it via stdin.

commands.name: server
commands.description: Run CSharpier as a server so that multiple files may be formatted.

options.name: --server-port
options.description: Specify the port that CSharpier should start on. Defaults to a random unused port.
